content.problemStatement:
You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, each of size NNN.

Let PPP denote a permutation length NNN. The permutation PPP is said to be good, if you can make an array CCC where:


Ci=Ai+BPiC_i = A_i + B_{P_i}Ci‚Äč=Ai‚Äč+BPi‚Äč‚Äč **or** Ci=Ai‚ąíBPiC_i = A_i - B_{P_i}Ci‚Äč=Ai‚Äč‚ąíBPi‚Äč‚Äč, for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1\le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N);
All elements of CCC are **equal**.
Out of the N!N!N! possible permutations, find the count of good permutations.

Since the count can be huge, output it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Note that a permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case consists of a single integer NNN - the size of arrays.
The second line of each test case consists of NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots , A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the elements of array A.
The third line of each test case consists of N space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2,\ldots , B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, the elements of array B.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the count of good permutations, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
